articles.title: Schools in Odishas’ Some Districts Shut From April 20 Amid Heat Wave | - odishabarta.com
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
Bureau,Odishabarta
Bhubaneswar: Schools and anganwadi centres across Odisha’s Khordha district will remain closed from April 20 to April 23 as authorities respond to intensifying heat wave conditions in the region.
The district administration issued the order as a precautionary measure to safeguard students from heat-related illnesses amid rising temperatures.
The directive, released by the District Collector, applies to all government and private educational institutions, including anganwadi centres.
Officials said the decision was taken keeping in view the safety and well-being of children, who are particularly vulnerable to extreme weather conditions.
The temporary closure aims to minimise exposure during peak daytime heat.
The move comes as several parts of Odisha continue to reel under severe heat, prompting local administrations to adopt preventive measures.
